tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; First, let's list all PagerDuty incidents to see what we're working with\n(def all-incidents (pagerduty__list_incidents {}))\n(count all-incidents)"}

text: Now I'll filter the incidents to find only those with HIGH urgency and "triggered" status:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Filter for high-urgency, triggered incidents
(def high-triggered 
  (filter (fn [inc]
            (and (= (:urgency inc) "high")
                 (= (:status inc) "triggered")))
          all-incidents))

;; Count and get their ids
(def count-high-triggered (count high-triggered))
(def ids (map :id high-triggered))

;; Return both the count and the list of ids
